one_liner: Photoreal macro miniature: a pop-up storybook page opening toward camera, every element die-cut cardstock assembled by tab-and-slot, real physical depth in perpendicular paper layers, fiber catching raking light at every cut edge.
render_law: Photoreal macro miniature paper-craft diorama — a pop-up storybook page opening flat toward camera. (1) MATERIAL: every element is die-cut cardstock (100-250gsm equivalent, visible fiber) assembled via tab-and-slot mechanisms, hinge folds and stacked layers; every prop is paper — characters, buildings, math tiles, arrows, plants — no clay, no plastic, no fabric. (2) EDGE + GRAIN: paper grain visible at raking light; cut edges show mild fiber tear at the knife stroke. (3) DEPTH: real physical depth — 2-4 layers of paper standing perpendicular to the page — never a painted illusion; the depth of field betrays the true miniature scale. (4) FOLD SHADOW: soft ambient-warm dark under folds, never black; warm bounce fill from below simulates page-glow. (5) LIGHT: warm 3200K key raking oblique to wake the fiber, a cool 5600K rim behind paper edges to backlight and halate the fiber. (6) CAMERA: low-angle side dolly or macro overhead, shallow DOF (f/2.8 macro equivalent) isolating the mechanism. IMPERATIVE: PHOTOREAL MACRO MINIATURE — real paper fiber, real cut edges, true macro depth of field. Forbid 2D cel shading, flat digital illustration, smooth full-CGI plastic surfaces, and any non-paper material on the built elements.
line_grammar: No drawn lines. Silhouettes carry via the crisp die-cut paper edge; interior detail is layered paper collage — shape on shape, never a printed line. Letters are raised die-cut cardstock, not printed type.
lens_grammar: 50-100mm macro. f/2.8-f/4 shallow DOF isolating the mechanism and betraying the miniature scale. Warm tungsten grade (3200K key). Mild 16mm-equivalent film grain. Slight barrel distortion allowed at the 50mm macro end.
light_law: Warm key from an oblique angle to rake the paper texture. Cool backlight from behind the paper edges for fiber halation. Bounce fill from an off-white foam-core equivalent. Shadow pockets under folds ambient-warm, never black.

palette_lock.bias: cream-kraft-terracotta dominant — warm paper-fiber tones catching the tungsten key, with construction-paper primary accents (crimson, cobalt, ochre) allowed where a beat needs a color pop. Shadow is warm paper-brown, never neutral-grey. NO neon, NO pastel-mint, NO cool digital white, NO CG plastic sheen.

motion_cadence: Slow deliberate mechanical motion — a tab slides in its slot, a page flap folds, a pop-up rises; every motion has a visible physical constraint. Hold 1.5-2s on the rest position, the fiber settling.
negative_lock: NO CGI 3D characters — all figures are built paper
NO Pixar-style bevel or subsurface skin
NO printed line-art on the paper — only cut shapes
NO fabric, NO clay, NO wood, NO plastic — paper only
NO named children's-book character (Curious George, Peter Rabbit, etc.)
NO 3D render, NO octane, NO realistic subsurface shading, NO 2.5D plastic look
Turkish label only — letters are raised die-cut paper, not printed, NO English signage
example_injection: Photoreal paper-craft pop-up diorama, low side dolly along the bottom page edge. A pop-up page lies open like a tiny room: a Mamilas paper figure (die-cut cardstock, tab-and-slot) stands mid-scene, with a folded paper element anchored at lower right and another at lower left, a narrow die-cut paper route line running between them. Warm 3200K low-sun key from the right strongly rakes the right corner; cool backlight halates the paper edges. Exactly one tactile note: clean cut-paper laminations show fiber at the corner tears. Turkish labels raised as die-cut cardstock words: 'BAŞLA' right, 'SON' left. Motion seed: the left flap one frame before folding shut. AVOID: CGI skin, English labels, printed line-art, warped Turkish letters, non-paper materials. Clean motion-ready start frame.

one_liner: Kurzgesagt flat-vector explainer: every shape a solid fill with a 1-2px darker outline (never black), a locked isometric diagram camera on a near-black navy field, and a single warm amber 'insight' glow blooming only at the moment of understanding.
render_law: Flat vector 2D explainer animation in the Kurzgesagt-in-a-Nutshell visual lineage. (1) SHAPES: every shape is a flat solid-color fill with a subtle 1-2px darker-tone outline (never pure black), zero gradient except the deliberate soft glow halo around the single 'insight' light source. (2) FORMS: characters and props are geometric-simplified — rounded blob and capsule forms, no detailed anatomy. (3) CAMERA: a locked orthographic-feeling overhead or three-quarter isometric view of a diagram or system, never a dramatic Dutch angle. (4) BACKGROUND: near-black navy-charcoal, uncluttered, so the diagram reads instantly. (5) COLOR-CODING is systemic — each conceptual element keeps one consistent flat color across the whole sequence so the audience tracks it without labels. (6) DEPTH: faked with layering and a soft drop-shadow, never true perspective or camera parallax. IMPERATIVE: FLAT 2D VECTOR ONLY — forbid painterly texture, 3D render, photoreal lighting, gradient shading beyond the single insight-glow bloom, and camera Dutch-tilt or handheld drift.
line_grammar: Thin 1-2px darker-tone outline (never pure black), uniform weight, no taper. Shapes read as flat geometric silhouettes first; there is no interior rendering, only flat color separation.
lens_grammar: Simulated locked overhead or 3/4 isometric orthographic view. No lens distortion, no depth of field, no camera move beyond the diagram's own reveal.
light_law: One soft amber 'insight' glow source per beat — everything else flat-lit with no directional shadow. The glow blooms softly at the moment of understanding and never elsewhere; it is a narrative event, not ambient light.

palette_lock.bias: a flat near-black navy-charcoal field holding the diagram, a single deep teal-cyan secondary for systemic color-coding, and one warm amber insight-accent that blooms only at the understanding beat. Color is flat information, not light — the only glow is the insight halo. NO gradient shading, NO painterly texture, NO photoreal falloff.

motion_cadence: The isometric diagram expands from center; one connector line draws once to the insight node; the amber glow lands and holds. The camera is locked wide overhead — the diagram's own reveal IS the motion event, not the camera.
negative_lock: NO Kurzgesagt bird mascot or any named explainer-channel character
NO photoreal texture, NO 3D render, NO painterly brushwork
NO gradient shading beyond the single insight-glow bloom
NO camera Dutch-tilt or handheld drift
NO octane, NO ray tracing, NO realistic lighting
Turkish label only — flat sans-serif diagram lettering, NO English signage
example_injection: Kurzgesagt-lineage flat vector explainer frame. Subject: a simplified geometric diagram of a concept, each element a flat solid-color shape with a 1-2px charcoal outline, arranged in a locked overhead isometric layout against a near-black navy field. One connector line draws once from the source node to the insight node; a soft amber glow blooms and holds at the insight node. Secondary deep teal-cyan elements stay flat and unlit. Turkish label 'KAVRAM' in flat geometric sans-serif beside the insight node. Motion seed: the connector line mid-draw, the glow about to bloom. AVOID: Kurzgesagt mascot, photoreal texture, 3D render, gradient shading, camera drift, English signage. Clean motion-ready start frame.

one_liner: RSA-Animate whiteboard: a pure cream board filling frame, confident pressure-variable graphite ink drawing itself in real time, one deliberate marker-wash color on the key node, and a real hand-and-marker visible at the active stroke edge.
render_law: Whiteboard/paper explainer animation in the RSA-Animate lineage. (1) SURFACE: a pure cream-white board fills the frame; drawings appear as if hand-inked in real time. (2) LINE: confident graphite-black ink of varying pressure — thin start, thicker press, thin lift — zero color except one deliberate marker-wash color used to highlight the key concept node. (3) NO SCENERY beyond the board itself. (4) THE HAND: a human hand and forearm holding a marker is visible at the active drawing edge, entering and leaving frame naturally — never a disembodied line. (5) BUILD: composition builds left-to-right or center-out as concepts accumulate, never erased mid-sequence unless the beat explicitly calls for a wipe. IMPERATIVE: FLAT 2D HAND-DRAWN INK ONLY — forbid 3D, painterly rendering, photoreal hand skin, gradient, and any color beyond the graphite ink and the single marker-wash accent.
line_grammar: Confident graphite-black ink, pressure-variable weight (thin-thick-thin per stroke), visible start and end taper, occasional natural wobble — never a perfect vector curve. All lettering matches the same hand-inked stroke grammar.
lens_grammar: Locked flat-on camera facing the board, no perspective distortion, no camera move beyond a slow reveal pull-back at completion.
light_law: Flat even board illumination, no directional shadow beyond the small contact shadow the hand and marker cast on the board surface.

palette_lock.bias: a paper-white board base carrying the whole frame, graphite-grey ink as the only line value, and a single brick-red marker-wash accent flooding the key node. Everything else is unlit flat board-white — the wash is the only saturation. NO gradient, NO 3D shading, NO photographic texture, NO second accent color.

motion_cadence: One ink stroke draws forward continuously; the marker-wash floods once behind the key node; the camera pulls back slowly as the diagram completes; the hand exits frame; hold on the completed board.
negative_lock: NO RSA Animate logo or branding
NO named real-world lecturer or speaker likeness
NO color beyond the graphite-black ink and the single marker-wash accent
NO 3D render, NO painterly shading, NO photoreal hand skin
NO gradient, NO octane, NO ray tracing
Turkish label only — hand-inked lettering matching the stroke grammar, NO English signage
example_injection: RSA-Animate-lineage whiteboard frame. Subject: a hand-inked diagram of a concept mid-draw on a cream-white board, confident graphite-black pressure-variable line, a human hand and marker visible at the active stroke edge entering from frame-right. One brick-red marker-wash floods behind the key concept node, everything else unlit flat board-white. Turkish label 'KAVRAM' hand-lettered beside the node in the same stroke grammar. Motion seed: the marker mid-stroke, the wash about to bloom. AVOID: RSA branding, named speaker likeness, extra color, 3D render, photoreal hand, English signage. Clean motion-ready start frame.

one_liner: Kinetic-poster flatness: solid flat color fills with zero gradient or texture, geometric shapes grid-snapped to precise alignment, 2-3 colors per frame for poster confidence, and a locked orthographic camera where the shape's snap IS the motion.
render_law: Flat motion-graphics / kinetic-poster design in the modern Saul-Bass-adjacent motion-design lineage. (1) FLATNESS: every element is a solid flat color fill — zero gradient, zero texture, zero simulated 3D shading — pure 2D graphic flatness. (2) GEOMETRY: shapes are geometric-precise (circles, rounded rectangles, exact angles) aligned to an implicit grid. (3) PALETTE: deliberately limited to 2-3 flat colors per frame for poster-level graphic confidence. (4) TYPE: bold geometric sans, grid-locked, when present. (5) SUBJECT: no character faces or organic anatomy — this world is for abstract, brand, and data-visualization subjects, not figures. IMPERATIVE: ABSOLUTE FLAT-COLOR GRAPHIC DISCIPLINE — forbid any gradient, any texture or grain, any 3D or photoreal element, soft-shadow simulation, and organic character anatomy.
line_grammar: No outline stroke needed — the shape edge is the silhouette itself, precise and vector-clean, no hand-drawn wobble. Where a stroke is used it is a flat solid weight, part of the geometry.
lens_grammar: Simulated flat orthographic 2D camera, dead square-on. No depth of field, no lens distortion, no perspective.
light_law: No directional lighting simulation at all — every flat fill is uniformly lit; value contrast comes only from the chosen flat colors, never from shading or falloff.

palette_lock.bias: flat solid-color fills only — a deep blue-charcoal ground, a warm near-white field, one bold vermilion-orange accent, clean white — a bold two-to-three-color scheme with poster flatness. Value contrast is the flat color choice itself, never shading. NO gradient, NO texture, NO grain, NO soft-shadow, NO more than three active colors.

motion_cadence: A bold geometric shape snaps to its final grid position; a secondary block slides into alignment; the camera is locked straight-on; no bounce — sharp snap-and-hold; 1s locked final state.
negative_lock: NO gradient anywhere, NO texture or grain, NO 3D or photoreal render
NO organic character anatomy — abstract, brand, or data subjects only
NO more than 3 flat colors active per frame
NO octane, NO ray tracing, NO soft-shadow simulation
Turkish label only — bold grid-locked geometric sans, NO English signage
example_injection: Flat motion-graphics frame. Subject: a bold flat vermilion-orange circle and a flat warm near-white rounded rectangle, precisely grid-aligned against a flat blue-charcoal ground — representing a concept as abstract geometry, zero gradient, zero texture. Bold geometric sans label 'VERİ' grid-locked beside the shapes. Motion seed: the rectangle mid-slide toward final alignment, about to snap. AVOID: gradients, texture, 3D, organic character forms, soft shadow, English signage. Clean motion-ready start frame.

one_liner: Japanese woodblock print grammar: flat carved color-fields with no blend, a confident black key-block outline, Prussian-blue dominant over aged cream paper, one red seal-stamp accent, and bold asymmetric wave-motif staging over visible paper grain.
render_law: Japanese ukiyo-e woodblock print in the Hokusai/Hiroshige-adjacent lineage (original composition only). (1) COLOR SEPARATION: flat printed color separation — each color sits in its own hand-carved-feeling flat field, no blended gradient, with a subtle woodgrain-texture bleed at the color-field edges. (2) LINE: a confident black key-block outline with a slightly irregular hand-carved edge quality, never digital-perfect. (3) PALETTE: Prussian-blue dominant with an aged cream-paper base and a single red seal-stamp accent. (4) COMPOSITION: bold graphic flatness with strong asymmetric diagonal or wave-motif staging; deep foreground/background layering achieved through flat color planes rather than photographic perspective. (5) SURFACE: visible paper-grain texture and soft age-toning over the whole print. IMPERATIVE: FLAT PRINTED WOODBLOCK AESTHETIC ONLY — forbid photographic gradient, 3D shading, digital airbrush blending, and modern glossy finish.
line_grammar: Confident black key-block outline with a slightly irregular hand-carved edge quality, no digital-perfect vector smoothness. Interior form stated by flat color-field boundaries, not by rendered line.
lens_grammar: Simulated flat print-plane composition — no camera lens simulated at all; this is a print, not a photographed or rendered scene. Bold asymmetric diagonal staging.
light_law: No directional lighting simulation — value comes from the flat printed color fields themselves, deepening only at each color-plane's own edge (a printed color-block, not a light falloff).

palette_lock.bias: Prussian-blue and aged-paper-cream dominant, a black woodblock key-line, a single madder-red seal-stamp accent. Color is carved flat pigment on paper, deepening only at plane edges — never a light gradient. NO photographic gradient, NO 3D shading, NO digital airbrush blend, NO modern saturation.

motion_cadence: A wave or pattern motif flows once across the frame in a single sinusoidal pulse; the Prussian-blue plane darkens once at the pulse's crest; no camera move — the stillness after the pulse is the closing state.
negative_lock: NO direct reproduction of a specific famous historic print (e.g. The Great Wave) — original composition in the grammar only
NO named ukiyo-e artist signature or seal text
NO photographic gradient, NO 3D render, NO digital airbrush blend
NO modern glossy finish, NO octane, NO ray tracing
Turkish label only — brush-carved woodblock-style lettering, NO English signage
example_injection: Ukiyo-e woodblock print frame. Subject: an original wave-motif composition, a Prussian-blue flat printed plane cresting behind a foreground element on aged cream-paper, a confident black key-block outline, a single madder-red seal-stamp accent in the upper corner. Visible paper-grain texture and age-toning over the whole print. Turkish label 'DALGA' in brush-carved woodblock lettering. Motion seed: the wave pulse mid-crest, about to darken and settle. AVOID: reproducing a specific famous historic print, named artist signature, photographic gradient, 3D render, English signage. Clean motion-ready start frame.

one_liner: Laika stop-motion: physical armature puppets with felted-fabric and silicone-skin micro-texture, genuine miniature sets in macro shallow focus, and a faint per-frame micro-strobe in the fine detail that must never be smoothed into digital tweening.
render_law: Stop-motion puppet animation in the Laika Studios theatrical lineage (grammar only, not the franchises). (1) PUPPET: every subject is a physical armature puppet with a visible tactile surface — felted fabric, silicone skin with pore-level micro-texture, hand-painted or fabric-sculpted detail, a faint fingerprint or tool-mark trace on soft surfaces. (2) SET: real miniatures — visible practical scale-model texture (the grain of built materials, tiny practical props), shot with genuine depth of field on a macro lens, never a flat digital background. (3) MICRO-STROBE: animation reads as true single-frame stop-motion — a faint per-frame micro-strobe/flicker in fine detail (hair, fabric fringe) from frame-by-frame physical repositioning; this is a FEATURE, never smoothed into digital tweening. (4) LIGHT: practical miniature-set lighting — small hot key lights, real shadow falloff at puppet scale. IMPERATIVE: PHYSICAL-PUPPET TACTILITY ONLY — forbid CG smoothness, digital cel shading, perfectly stable digital-interpolated motion, and flat digital backgrounds.
line_grammar: N/A — physical puppet, not line-based. The silhouette is defined by the sculpted or felted puppet surface itself, a soft practical edge, never a hard vector or ink outline.
lens_grammar: Macro-to-standard practical lens simulation (35-50mm equivalent at miniature scale), genuine shallow depth of field on the tiny set that betrays its true scale, slight practical lens grain.
light_law: Small hot practical key lights at puppet scale, real falloff and real shadow softness proportional to the miniature set size. No digital ambient-occlusion fake — shadows come from an actual light-source position on the built set.

palette_lock.bias: warm handmade-material palette — felt, wood, painted resin — earthy tactile tones catching a small hot practical key. Shadow is warm-brown material shadow, never neutral-grey. NO digital-clean saturation, NO CG plastic sheen, NO flat cel color, NO cool digital grade.

motion_cadence: The puppet moves in true single-frame stop-motion increments — a faint per-frame micro-strobe in fine surface detail (hair, fabric) each hold. One physical gesture per beat, settling into a tactile final hold with a last faint frame-flicker before stillness.
negative_lock: NO Coraline, NO Kubo, NO Norman, NO any named Laika franchise character or creature design
NO CG smoothness, NO digital cel shading, NO perfectly stable interpolated motion — must read as true stop-motion
NO flat digital background — the miniature set must read as physically built
NO octane, NO ray tracing, NO plastic CG skin
Turkish label only — hand-crafted physical-prop lettering (carved, painted, or fabric-appliquéd), NO English signage
example_injection: Laika-lineage stop-motion frame. Subject: an original felted-fabric Mamilas puppet mid-close-up, visible fabric-grain and hand-stitched seam detail, silicone-skin micro-texture on the exposed hands, standing in a genuine miniature set with practical scale-model props in shallow macro depth of field. One small hot key light casts a real falloff shadow across the tactile set floor. Warm handmade palette — felt walnut-browns, a painted copper-resin accent. Turkish label 'MASAL' carved into a tiny wooden sign prop in the background. Motion seed: the puppet mid-gesture, a faint per-frame strobe about to settle into the tactile final hold. AVOID: named Laika characters, CG smoothness, digital cel shading, flat background, English signage. Clean motion-ready start frame.

one_liner: Aardman plasticine: hand-sculpted clay photographed on a real miniature set, fingerprints and tool marks in the surface, wide expressive mouths and sculpted brow strips as the acting engine, and a true 12fps stop-motion stagger with soft edge-boil.
render_law: Physical plasticine stop-motion in the Aardman workshop lineage — every character and prop reads as hand-sculpted clay photographed on a real miniature set. (1) SURFACE RECORD: the full tactile record is present — fingerprints pressed into cheeks, tool marks at seams, a faint sheen where a warm thumb smoothed the clay. (2) IMPERFECTION: shapes are softly imperfect — no two eyes exactly level, the mouth re-sculpted between poses so lip shapes vary frame to frame (edge boil). (3) ACTING ENGINE: the wide mouth and heavy brow — huge expressive mouth shapes, eyebrows as separate sculpted clay strips. (4) SET: real-material miniatures — painted cardboard walls, fabric curtains with visibly wrong-scale weave, wire-and-foam trees. (5) LIGHT + PALETTE: warm pigment palette (warm terracotta clay mid, brick-red accent) under soft scaled-down studio light; miniature physics — the depth of field betrays the set's true ~40cm scale. IMPERATIVE: PHOTOGRAPHED MINIATURE PLASTICINE — forbid smooth CGI perfection, digital gloss, 60fps interpolation, real human skin, and drawn outlines or cel shading.
line_grammar: No drawn lines. Silhouettes carry through physical light on the sculpted form; the seam where two clay colors meet is a real modeled ridge, slightly proud of the surface. Eye highlights are tiny glued beads catching the key light.
lens_grammar: Macro tabletop photography: 50-60mm at f/2.8-f/4 so the miniature set falls into creamy defocus and betrays its true scale. Camera height at character eye-level, ~20cm off the deck. Gentle push-ins on emotional beats, executed as physical dolly moves with a tiny mechanical imperfection.
light_law: Scaled-down studio three-point: a soft warm key through diffusion, gentle fill, small practical lamps glowing amber inside the set. Shadows soft-edged and shallow. Thin clay parts pick up a subtle warm translucency — ears glow faintly when backlit.

palette_lock.bias: warm earthy physical-pigment palette — clay-brown shadow, warm terracotta clay mid, brick-red accent, warm cream highlight — under a soft diffused studio key. Colors are mixed pigment in real clay, catching real falloff. NO digital-clean saturation, NO CG plastic gloss, NO cool grade, NO flat cel color.

motion_cadence: True 12fps stop-motion stagger — motion arrives in discrete sculpted poses, never interpolated. Edges boil softly because the clay is re-handled between frames. Blinks are two-frame lid swaps. Fast actions use sculpted clay smears — an arm stretched into a physical blur shape held for 1-2 frames.
negative_lock: NO Wallace, NO Gromit, NO Shaun the Sheep, NO Feathers McGraw, NO Chicken Run identity
NO smooth 60fps interpolation, NO digital motion blur — only sculpted clay smears
NO perfect CGI surface, NO digital glow, NO photoreal subsurface skin
NO drawn outline, NO cel shading
NO real human hands or scale reference in frame
Turkish label only — sculpted clay letters or hand-painted miniature signs, NO English signage
example_injection: Photographed miniature plasticine frame. Subject: a round-cheeked Mamilas clay character at a tiny wooden desk, a thumbprint visible on the left cheek, bead-highlight eyes looking up mid-thought, mouth sculpted into a wide 'o'. Warm diffused key from a window off left, an amber practical desk lamp glowing in-set. Background: a painted cardboard classroom wall falling into creamy macro defocus, a fabric curtain with too-large weave. Sculpted clay letters on the desk edge spell 'FEN' in brick-red. Motion seed: the character's hand hovers one frame from grabbing a clay pencil; edge boil already alive on the sleeve. AVOID: Wallace, Gromit, CGI smoothness, 60fps, drawn outlines, English labels. Clean motion-ready start frame.

one_liner: Children's-book watercolor on cold-press paper: washes bleeding wet-into-wet with feathered blooms, a loose pencil underdrawing that the paint drifts off-register from, reserved warm paper as the only highlight, and a shadow mixed as blue-grey wash — never black.
render_law: Traditional watercolor on cold-press paper in the children's storybook lineage — every frame is a painting whose paper is always felt. (1) WASHES: bleed wet-into-wet at every soft boundary — a sky flows into a hill with a feathered bloom edge; pigment granulates in the valleys of the paper tooth. (2) UNDERDRAWING: a loose pencil underdrawing stays honest and visible, and the color washes drift off-register from it — the paint does not obey the pencil, it converses with it. (3) HIGHLIGHTS: reserved warm paper, never white paint. (4) EDGES do double duty — crisp where the brush ran dry, cauliflower blooms where water pooled. (5) PALETTE: soft and breathable — dusty blue, warm coral, a shadow mixed as a blue-grey wash, never black. (6) FORM + SCALE: objects simplify toward toy-like warmth; scale may gently exaggerate (big moon, small house). White gouache flecks allowed only for stars, snow, dandelion seeds. IMPERATIVE: WET WATERCOLOR ON VISIBLE PAPER — forbid digital gradients, opaque solid fills, hard vector edges, 3D CGI depth, and pure black.
line_grammar: Loose graphite pencil line, sketchy and searching — two or three light strokes finding a contour rather than one confident line. Ink used sparingly for eyes and tiny details. Lines and washes intentionally misaligned by a few millimeters.
lens_grammar: Flat illustrated staging with gentle atmospheric perspective — distance fades into paler, wetter washes, never photographic blur. Compositions breathe with paper margins; intimate beats float as vignettes on bare white paper.
light_law: Light lives in pigment density: shadow is a second wet wash dropped over a dry first pass, glowing at its feathered edge. Sunlight is warm paper left bare. No cast-shadow geometry — shadows are soft pooled blobs anchoring the feet.

palette_lock.bias: soft desaturated washes — a mixed blue-grey shadow (never black), dusty-blue mid, warm coral accent, and reserved warm-paper highlight. Pigment granulates in the paper tooth; the darkest value is a wet blue-grey wash. NO opaque solid fill, NO digital gradient ramp, NO hard vector edge, NO pure black, NO saturated primary.

motion_cadence: Gentle floating motion — elements drift and settle like paper cutouts on water. Transitions dissolve as one wash bleeding into the next. Nothing snaps; every arrival has a soft overshoot and settle. Two-second holds on painted stillness.
negative_lock: NO 3D CGI, NO photographic depth of field
NO opaque solid fill, NO digital gradient ramp, NO hard vector edge
NO black — the darkest value is a mixed blue-grey wash
NO neon, NO saturated primary color
NO Beatrix Potter or known picture-book character identity
Turkish label only — hand-lettered with a fine brush, a slightly wobbly baseline, NO English signage
example_injection: Wet watercolor storybook frame on cold-press paper. Subject: a small Mamilas child figure in a warm-coral raincoat under an oversized dusty-blue umbrella, the pencil underdrawing visible and slightly off-register from the paint. The puddle at the boots is one wet wash with a cauliflower bloom edge. Background: a hill bleeding into a rain sky wet-into-wet, a distant house fading to a paler wash, paper tooth granulating across the sky. Reserved-paper highlights along the umbrella rim. Hand-lettered 'BAHAR' floats in the top margin with a wobbly brush baseline. Motion seed: one raindrop ring just opening in the puddle. AVOID: CGI, opaque fills, vector edges, pure black, English lettering. Clean motion-ready start frame.
